faq.title: Common
faq.description: Answers to questions prospects ask before booking.

clients.question: What kind of businesses do you work with?
clients.answer: Established businesses where the operation has outgrown the tools holding it together. The trigger is usually loss of control, visibility, or flow. If the business is pre-revenue or still figuring out its process, the timing is wrong.

audit.question: What does the Systems Audit involve?
audit.answer: A structured conversation that maps your current operation, identifies where work is falling through, and defines what to build and in what order. It costs 300 euros and takes one session. If the project proceeds, the 300 euros is deducted from the build cost.

custom.question: Why build something custom instead of using existing SaaS tools?
custom.answer: SaaS works well when your problem is standard. When your process is specific to how you actually operate, off-the-shelf tools require you to work around them. You end up paying for features you do not use and building manual workarounds for the gaps. A focused custom system fixes the specific problem and nothing else.

wordpress.question: Do you work with WordPress or Framer?
wordpress.answer: No. If that is what you need, I will point you to someone who does it well.

timeline.question: How long does a project take?
timeline.answer: Depends on scope. A focused operations system typically runs four to eight weeks. A full SaaS build runs eight to twelve weeks. Nothing starts without a written scope and a deposit.

afterBuild.question: What happens after the build?
afterBuild.answer: The system is yours. Fully documented, transferable to any developer. I offer maintenance retainers for clients who want ongoing support, but there is no lock-in.

cta.title: If you can describe what is breaking, I can describe what would fix it.
cta.description: Before anything is quoted, I map how your system actually works. Then we decide together if there is something worth building.
cta.note: Another developer can take over without rebuilding everything.

remote.question: Do you work with clients outside of France?
remote.answer: Yes. I work remotely across Europe and beyond. Communication via video calls, email, and your preferred tools.

takeover.question: Can you take over an existing project?
takeover.answer: Absolutely. I audit the current state, identify issues, and propose a plan to move forward.

metadata.title: About: Independent Engineering Practice
metadata.description: I build the systems established businesses need to keep working as they grow. Not websites. Not templates. Systems that show where work is, what is blocked, and what needs to happen next.

hero.title: About
hero.description: I build the systems established businesses need to keep working as they grow.

intro.paragraph1: Deralis Digital is an independent engineering practice. I work with profitable, operational businesses where the way information moves between people and tools has become the bottleneck. Not websites. Not templates. Systems that show where work is, what is blocked, and what needs to happen next.
intro.paragraph2: Before Deralis, I worked inside equipment rental operations at Loxam and Ariane Systems. That experience produced TraviXO, a VGP compliance SaaS built specifically for the French equipment rental industry. That experience taught me one thing that shapes every engagement: you cannot design a system for an operation you do not understand.

philosophy.title: My philosophy
philosophy.subtitle: The system must survive without you.
philosophy.conclusion: Every system I build is transferable. Documented. Maintainable by another developer. You own the system. There is no lock-in.

whatWeBuild.title: What I build
whatWeBuild.description: I specialize in full-stack platform development.

saas.title: Operations management systems
saas.description: Web-based systems your team can access from anywhere, with logins, permissions, and recurring billing if you need it.

dashboards.title: Team dashboards
dashboards.description: One screen that shows the whole team where work is, what is blocked, and what needs attention. With proper logins and role-based visibility.

databases.title: Reliable data foundations
databases.description: The structured storage your operation needs, designed to grow with you and not lose data along the way.

automation.title: Process automation
automation.description: Connecting your existing tools so information moves between them without anyone copy-pasting. Triggers, scheduled tasks, integrations.

api.title: Tool connections
api.description: When two tools need to talk to each other but don't, I build the bridge.

whatWeBuild.note: Each system I build is designed for the specific operation it serves. No templates. No copy-pasted solutions.

howWeWork.title: How I work
howWeWork.description: Clients work directly with me, without layers, outsourcing, or miscommunication. The process is simple and technical:

step1.title: Discovery
step1.description: I sit with you and walk through how your operation actually works today, where time gets lost, and where the breakage hurts most.

step2.title: Design
step2.description: I sketch the system: what it does, what it shows, what happens when something goes wrong, how it grows with you.

step3.title: Build
step3.description: I write the code, set up the database, and build the interface. You see it as it takes shape, not at the end.

step4.title: Deploy
step4.description: I get it onto a live server, test it under real conditions, and walk you through how to use it.

step5.title: Support
step5.description: I am still around. Updates, fixes, additions, or a clean handover to another developer when you want one.

howWeWork.note: This workflow ensures stable delivery and reduces time-to-production.

techExpertise.title: Technology expertise
techExpertise.description: I use a modern, unified stack to keep development fast, predictable, and scalable.

screenshots.title: Real systems I have built
screenshots.description: Dashboards, workflows, and database systems used in production environments.

notes.note1: These systems include multi-tenant logic, role-based access, SQL databases, and automation workflows.
notes.note2: All platforms shown here are built with real databases, real logic, and real engineering, not templates.
notes.note3: This demonstrates my focus: systems, not pages.

principle.label: Principle
principle.text: No build begins without a shared definition of done. Getting there is the work that keeps you from paying for the wrong thing.

whyChoose.title: Why businesses choose Deralis Digital
items: You work directly with the person building it. No project manager, no junior handoff, no sales layer.
One person responsible for everything. The interface, the logic, the data, the deployment.
Built with current tools so the next developer who touches it can read it and continue.
whyChoose.conclusion: Deralis Digital is built for businesses that want their operation to run, not their software to impress.

location.title: Where I operate
location.description: Based in France. Working with clients worldwide.

web-system-not-just-a-website.excerpt: A website shows people you exist. A web system makes your business run. Here is the difference, and how to know which one you actually need.

metadata.title: Privacy Policy
metadata.description: Privacy Policy for Deralis Digital. How I collect, use, and protect your personal data.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Deralis Digital is an independent engineering practice providing web development and digital services. This Privacy Policy explains how personal data is collected, used, and protected when you visit deralis.digital or contact Deralis Digital.

voluntary.title: 1.1 Information you submit voluntarily
voluntary.intro: When you contact Deralis Digital through email or forms, the following is collected:
voluntary.note: This information is used solely to respond to your inquiry.

technical.title: 1.2 Technical data
technical.intro: When you browse the website, basic technical information may be collected automatically:
items: Date, time, and duration of visit
technical.note: This data is used for security, performance monitoring, and aggregated analytics.
technical.noTrackers: This site does not use advertising trackers, third-party marketing cookies, or profiling tools.

howWeUse.title: 2. How your data is used
howWeUse.intro: Your data is used only for:
howWeUse.noSell: Deralis Digital does not sell, rent, or trade your personal data.

legalBasis.title: 3. Legal basis (GDPR)
legalBasis.intro: Personal data is processed based on:

items.legitimate: Legitimate interest: responding to inquiries, improving website performance
items.contract: Performance of a contract: when working together
items.consent: Consent: if you choose to provide information

storage.title: 4. Data storage and retention
storage.intro1: Personal data is stored securely in the EU or equivalent jurisdictions.
storage.intro2: Data is retained only as long as necessary:

items.project: Project information: duration of the project + legal obligations
items.analytics: Analytics logs: typically 30 to 90 days depending on provider

sharing.title: 5. Sharing your data
sharing.compliance: These providers comply with GDPR or offer equivalent protections.
sharing.noMarketing: Your information is not shared with third parties for marketing.

rights.title: 6. Your rights (EU & GDPR)
items: Request a copy of your data
rights.exercise: You may exercise these rights at:

cookies.title: 7. Cookies
cookies.noAds: This site does not use advertising cookies or trackers.

contact.title: 8. Contact
contact.intro: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, contact:

metadata.title: Terms of Service
metadata.description: Terms of Service for Deralis Digital. Conditions for using the website and services.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms govern your use of the website deralis.digital and any services provided by Deralis Digital. By accessing the website or making contact, you agree to these Terms.

websiteUse.title: 1. Use of the website
websiteUse.intro: You may browse this website for informational purposes. You agree not to:
items: Attempt to breach the website's security
Copy or reproduce content without permission
Misuse contact forms or attempt spam

services.title: 2. Services
services.note: A service relationship begins only after both parties agree in writing to a proposal or contract.

quotes.title: 3. Quotes & pricing
items: Are valid for 30 days unless stated otherwise
May change based on updated requirements
Are not binding until formally accepted

payment.title: 4. Payment terms
payment.intro: Specific payment terms are defined in each project contract. Unless otherwise stated:
items: Development projects may use milestone-based payments
Work begins after initial payment is received
payment.late: Late payments may result in suspension of services.

ip.title: 5. Intellectual property
items: Deralis Digital retains ownership of development tools, internal libraries, and frameworks used.
The client receives full usage rights for all custom deliverables created specifically for their project.
Third-party libraries remain licensed under their respective terms.

confidentiality.title: 6. Confidentiality
confidentiality.content: All project information shared between you and Deralis Digital is confidential and will not be disclosed without consent, except when legally required.

liability.title: 7. Limitation of liability
liability.intro: Deralis Digital is not liable for:
items: Issues caused by third-party hosting providers, APIs, or services
liability.limit: Liability is limited to the amount paid for services in the preceding 3 months.

termination.title: 8. Termination
termination.content: Both parties may terminate a project contract with written notice if obligations are not met. Completed deliverables and pending payments remain due.

law.title: 9. Governing law
law.content: These Terms are governed by French law. Any dispute shall be handled by the competent courts in France.
